title: EvidenceCard
description: A semi-structured evidence card extracted from one or more research papers, grounding a causal pathway in a diagnosis framework with qualitative and quantitative conditions drawn from literature.

production_system.description: Top-level production system from the diagnosis framework

observed_stress.description: The observed community-level stress this card helps diagnose. Must match a key in diagnosis_framework.json

causal_pathway.description: The specific causal pathway this card grounds. Must match a key in diagnosis_framework.json

context.description: Describes the agro-ecological and socio-economic context in which the paper's findings apply. Used for retrieval filtering.

agro_climatic_zones.description: Agro-climatic zones where this evidence applies (ICAR classification or FAO zone names). Use ['any'] if broadly applicable.

rainfall_regime.description: Rainfall regime where the paper's conditions apply

crop_systems.description: Relevant crop systems mentioned in study (optional).

geographic_examples.description: Specific regions, states, or countries in which the source studies were conducted.

diagnostic_signals.description: List of diagnostic signals drawn from the paper. Each signal maps one or more variables to a condition expression, with a severity level and qualitative explanation. These are the building blocks for LLM reasoning.

signal_id.description: Short unique label within the card. Example: sig_01

variables.description: Variable names from data_dictionary.json involved in this signal.

condition.description: The logical condition grounded in the paper. Use 'expression' for quantitative thresholds where available; 'qualitative' where the paper only gives directional or descriptive guidance.

type.description: quantitative: a threshold expression | qualitative: directional description only | trend: change over time | composite: combination of multiple sub-conditions

expression.description: Python-evaluable logical expression string using variable names. Use None if type is qualitative. Example: 'soge_dev_percent > 70 and aquifer_class == "hard_rock"'

qualitative_description.description: Human-readable description of the condition when a precise threshold is unavailable. Required if type is qualitative or trend.
examples: Well depth is increasing year-on-year over a multi-year window, indicating declining water table
Barren land area has increased relative to baseline cropland area
Monsoon onset is delayed by more than 2 weeks compared to historical average

threshold_confidence.description: Confidence in the threshold value: high = from peer-reviewed empirical study with similar context; medium = from expert guidelines or grey literature; low = extrapolated from dissimilar context; none = no quantitative threshold available

context_sensitivity.description: How context-sensitive the threshold is: global = applies broadly; regional = applies to similar agro-climatic zones; local = should only be used in the specific study context

severity.description: Severity of the causal pathway if this signal is active, as assessed from literature

direction.description: confirms: strong evidence this cause is active | suggests: weak or indirect evidence | rules_out: variable value makes this cause unlikely | amplifies: strengthens another active signal when co-occurring

explanation.description: One to three sentence plain-language explanation of why this variable pattern indicates the causal pathway, drawing from the paper's findings.

interaction_with.description: Signal IDs within the same card (or across cards in the same pathway) that interact with this signal — i.e. the combination of both signals is more or less diagnostic than each alone.

overall_reasoning_note.description: A paragraph-length note for the LLM summarising how to reason over the signals in this card collectively — which signals are sufficient alone, which need to co-occur, how to handle missing variables, and what alternative causes to rule out first.

confounders.description: Alternative explanations or confounding conditions that this evidence card cannot rule out on its own, and which the LLM should consider before concluding the causal pathway is active.

confounder.description: The alternative causal pathway or confounding variable

how_to_distinguish.description: Which variable or user question can help distinguish this confounder from the primary pathway

missing_variable_questions.description: Targeted questions to ask the user when key not-available variables are missing, in priority order. Used by the interactive system to elicit data that would most improve diagnostic confidence.

missing_variable.description: Variable name from data_dictionary.json with availability: not_available

question_to_user.description: Plain-language question phrased for a non-technical user (farmer, extension worker, NGO staff)

how_answer_updates_diagnosis.description: How the user's answer changes the diagnostic conclusion or confidence level

sources.description: Research papers, reports, or guidelines from which the evidence in this card is drawn.

relevance_note.description: One sentence on which specific finding from this source grounds the signal(s) in this card

metadata.description: Card-level metadata for versioning and quality tracking

created_by.description: Pipeline stage or human reviewer that created this card. E.g. 'llm_extraction_v1', 'expert_review'

extraction_model.description: LLM model used for extraction if created by pipeline. E.g. claude-sonnet-4-20250514

confidence_overall.description: Overall confidence in this card's diagnostic utility, assessed at creation or review time
